title: Aster pansus
taxonomy: Aster pansus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Aster, which includes various species of daisies and asters. The plant's classification is further supported by its botanical name, Aster pansus, indicating its membership in the Aster genus. Aster pansus is native to the mountainous regions of North America, where it grows as a perennial herb. As a member of the Asteraceae family, Aster pansus shares characteristics with other daisy-like plants, including its composite flower heads.

intro: Aster paniculatus, commonly known as Kalmegh or Kalmeegh, is a plant species native to India and other parts of Asia, belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and hepatoprotective effects, which contribute to its potential health benefits in treating various ailments such as fever, digestive issues, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Aster paniculatus include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used to prepare various herbal preparations, including decoctions, infusions, and capsules, which are often marketed as supplements to support liver health and immune function.
